title: Stop Building AI Features. Start Solving Problems.
excerpt: Most businesses don't need AI. They need a solution to a problem. Sometimes AI is that solution. Often it isn't.

title: Why Your AI Proof of Concept Worked but Your Production System Doesn't
excerpt: The gap between a working demo and a reliable production AI system is wider than most teams expect. Here's where it breaks.

title: You Don't Have an AI Problem. You Have a Data Problem.
excerpt: Most AI initiatives fail not because of the model but because the data underneath it is a mess. Fix the foundation first.

title: Context Windows Are a Lie (Sort Of)
excerpt: Why a 200K token context window doesn't mean your AI can actually use 200K tokens of information effectively.

title: The Guardrails Problem: When Your AI Needs to Be Told No
excerpt: AI without guardrails is a liability. But most guardrail implementations are either too loose or too restrictive. Here's how to get it right.

title: Your AI Doesn't Remember You. Here's Why That Matters.
excerpt: Why most AI assistants forget everything between sessions, and how a six-layer memory system changes the game.

title: Planner, Worker, Synthesizer: The 3-Agent Pattern That Actually Works
excerpt: How breaking one AI agent into three separate agents eliminated tool call hallucinations and made the system dramatically more reliable.

title: When Your AI Agent Needs a Costume Change vs. a Whole New Actor
excerpt: The architectural decision a lot of AI builders get wrong — when to tune an agent's configuration vs. when to build a specialized one entirely.
